relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: After belovedness is named, Jesus is led into wilderness testing.
Forty days of fasting bring him to hunger and vulnerability.
The tester presses identity through appetite: prove sonship by turning stones to bread.
Jesus answers that life is fed by more than bread; it lives from the Divine word.
The test moves to holy spectacle and temple height.
Scripture itself is used as a pressure point for false proving.
Jesus refuses to test God in the name of trust.
The test widens to worldly realms and visible glory.
Power is offered at the cost of misdirected worship.
Jesus dismisses the adversarial offer and centers service to the Divine alone.
The tester leaves, and messengers serve him.
John’s arrest shifts Jesus toward Galilee.
He dwells by the sea in the old tribal lands.
Isaiah’s memory begins to unfold.
Galilee of the nations becomes the place of light.
A people sitting in darkness see great light rise.
Jesus begins the proclamation: turn and realign, heaven’s reign is near.
By the sea, he sees working brothers.
He calls them from catching fish into gathering people.
They leave the nets immediately.
Another pair of brothers is called from boat and father.
They too leave and follow.
Teaching, proclamation, and healing become the movement’s first public shape.
Suffering people are brought to him, and healing spreads.
Crowds begin following from every direction.
